dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, the {enemy_type} know we're coming for them. And they've responded the only way they know how.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: They killed another one. A shepherd boy, barely twelve years old, found at the edge of {location} this morning with their mark carved into a post beside him. {target_npc} is trying to keep the settlement from turning into a mob, but the rage is boiling over. If we don't strike now, the villagers will charge in themselves and get massacred. You need to hit the {enemy_type} lair {location_hint} before this grief turns into a bloodbath on both sides.
dialogueChunks.outro: Every day we wait, they take another life to prove they can. This has to end.

playerResponses.text: A child? No more waiting. I'm going now.
They're provoking us into a mistake. I'll stay focused and hit them where it hurts.
This cycle of killing will never stop. I can't be part of it.

dialogueChunks.intro: We found something at the {enemy_type} camp you raided, {player_name}. Something that changes everything.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Belongings. Personal items from at least a dozen victims, kept as trophies. My brother's ring was among them. {target_npc} identified their sister's pendant. Three other families in {location} found pieces of people they'd lost. The {enemy_type} didn't just kill our people: they collected souvenirs. {target_npc} says there's a deeper warren {location_hint} where the leader keeps their hoard. We need you to recover what you can and destroy the rest.
dialogueChunks.outro: Those items belong to the dead, and to the families who mourn them. They should never have been trophies.

playerResponses.text: Trophies from the dead? I'll bring everything back and make them pay.
Deeper into their territory for personal effects? The families need this. I'll go.
Going deeper into their lair alone is asking to join the dead. I won't do it.

dialogueChunks.intro: Bad news, {player_name}. The {enemy_type} have found allies.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: A second group has joined the ones you've been hunting: drawn by the commotion, or maybe called in. {target_npc} spotted them arriving {location_hint} in numbers we haven't seen before. They've doubled the guard around their main camp and they're raiding more aggressively, like they're gathering {quest_item} to fortify for a prolonged conflict. If we don't break their alliance now, {location} will face a force too large for any rescue to matter.
dialogueChunks.outro: Cut their supply line, scatter the reinforcements, and finish what we started. Before it grows beyond anyone's ability to stop.

playerResponses.text: Reinforcements change nothing. I'll cut through them all.
Double the enemies means double the danger. I'll need more resources.
An enemy that's growing stronger while I fight alone? The odds are impossible.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I need you to listen carefully. Some of our own people are wavering.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Half the settlement thinks we should negotiate with the {enemy_type} instead of fighting. They say the killing has to stop, and they're not wrong about that: but negotiation means accepting what was done to our families. {target_npc} is holding the community together, but barely. To prove that this fight is worth finishing, we need a decisive strike. There's a forward outpost {location_hint} where the {enemy_type} have been stockpiling {quest_item}. Destroy it, and we show {location} that victory is within reach.
dialogueChunks.outro: If the settlement loses its nerve now, everyone who died will have died for nothing. We need this win.

playerResponses.text: A decisive strike to restore their courage. I'll make it count.
Internal divisions on top of external enemies? I'll handle the outpost, you handle the politics.
If your own people want peace, maybe you should listen to them.
